title: coelogyne pumila var. recurva
intro: Coelogyne pumila var. recurva is a rare and unique species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Asia, characterized by its compact and upright growth habit, with small white flowers that bloom in clusters. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Coelogyne pumila var. recurva are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
